title.zh: 南山三列山系的鸟龙之神
title.en: Bird-and-Dragon Guardians of the Three Southern Ranges

summary.zh: 南山经三列山系的集体山神：鹊山十山之神鸟身龙首，次二经十七山之神龙身鸟首，次三经十四山之神龙身人面，各有祠礼。
summary.en: The collective mountain gods of the three southern ranges: bird-bodied and dragon-headed in the first, dragon-bodied and bird-headed in the second, dragon-bodied and human-faced in the third, each with prescribed rites.

description.zh:
《南山经》的四十一座山分作三列山系，每列的结尾都停下来交代一件事：这一路的山神长什么样，该怎么祭。自招摇之山至箕尾之山的鹊山十山，"其神状皆鸟身而龙首"；自柜山至漆吴之山的十七山，"其神状皆龙身而鸟首"；自天虞之山至南禺之山的十四山，"其神皆龙身而人面"。鸟与龙两种部件在三列山系间翻转拼装，越往南，龙身越占上风，最后换上一张人脸。

祭法各有等差。鹊山之神："毛用一璋玉瘗，糈用稌米，一璧稻米、白菅为席"——以带毛的牲与一块璋玉埋入地下，祭米用稌稻，再陈一璧，铺白菅草为席。次二经之神毛用一璧瘗，糈用稌；次三经之神则"皆一白狗祈，糈用稌"，用一只白狗祷祭。瘗是埋祭，山神在地不在天，祭品要埋进土里才算送达。

这些山神没有名字，没有事迹，也没有神殿，只有形貌与应得的祭品清单，像一份写给行旅与祭司的公文。正是这类段落让历代学者把《山经》读作上古巫祝的祭祀地理手册——比起讲故事，它更关心在哪座山、该埋下什么。
description.en:
The forty-one mountains of the Classic of the Southern Mountains fall into three ranges, and at the end of each range the itinerary pauses for the same two matters: what the gods of these mountains look like, and what they are owed. The ten mountains from Zhaoyao to Jiwei have gods with birds' bodies and dragons' heads; the seventeen from Ju to Qiwu, dragons' bodies and birds' heads; the fourteen from Tianyu to Nanyu, dragons' bodies and human faces. Bird and dragon are recombined range by range, the dragon gaining ground southward until it finally wears a human face.

The rites are graded. The gods of the first range receive a sacrificial animal buried with a zhang scepter of jade, an offering of tu rice, a bi disk, and a mat of white rushes; those of the second, a buried bi disk and tu rice; those of the third, the prayer of a single white dog with tu rice. Burial is the operative act — these gods dwell in the earth, not the sky, and offerings reach them only by being put into the ground.

The gods have no names, no deeds, no temples: only bodies and a tariff of dues, like a circular issued to travelers and officiants. Passages such as these are why scholars have long read the Classic of Mountains less as storytelling than as a ritual geography — a handbook of which mountain requires what, buried where.

image.sourceTitle: Imperial Encyclopaedia - Spirits and the Supernatural - pic11 - 天虞山至南禺山共十四山之神圖
